data.title: BNB Chain Extends Zero-Fee Transfers for USDC and USD1
data.description: According to BlockBeats, an official announcement revealed that the zero-fee transfer promotion for USDC and USD1 on the BNB Chain has been extended until December 31. This initiative aims to encourage more transactions and increase user engagement on the platform.

data.title: Crypto News Today: Crypto Sentiment Rebounds to Levels Last Seen When Bitcoin Traded Above $100K
data.description: Crypto market sentiment is showing early signs of recovery as Bitcoin holds above $90,000 — a notable shift from earlier this month when sentiment was weaker despite BTC trading above $100,000.The Crypto Fear & Greed Index rose to 25 (“Extreme Fear”) on Friday, up three points from Thursday and nearly ten points higher than on Nov. 13 — the last day Bitcoin traded above $100,000 before sliding into its recent correction.Bitcoin is trading at $91,032 at the time of publication, according to CoinMarketCap, sparking fresh debate among analysts about whether the market can reclaim six figures before year-end.Sentiment Volatility Returns as Traders Debate Short-Term DirectionAnalyst Ted wrote on X Thursday that a decisive reclaim of $93,000–$94,000 could allow Bitcoin to retest $100,000 before any renewed downside.Meanwhile, on-chain analytics firm Santiment highlighted an uptick in bearish sentiment across social platforms — a historically contrarian signal that often precedes market reversals.“Most major turnarounds occur when retail’s hope is mainly lost,” Santiment stated, noting that crypto markets often move opposite the crowd’s expectations.Bitcoin remains down 18.9% over the past 30 days, according to CoinMarketCap.Even long-time bulls are moderating expectations. On Thursday, BitMine chairman Tom Lee softened his longstanding call for $250,000 BTC by year-end, acknowledging that a return to $100,000 — and maybe a new all-time high above $125,100 — is now a more realistic outcome.Will December break from Bitcoin’s historical patterns?Crypto trader Jelle noted that “after a bunch of slow-bleed corrections, almost everyone was caught off guard by the sell-off,” adding that BTC now enters December in a vulnerable but potentially opportunistic position.Historically, December has been a moderately positive month for Bitcoin:Average December return since 2013: +4.75% (CoinGlass)However, both October and November — two of Bitcoin’s strongest months historically — have underperformed sharply in 2025. This divergence has left traders questioning whether December’s seasonal strength will hold, or whether BTC may continue facing macro-driven volatility.Bottom LineCrypto sentiment is recovering despite Bitcoin trading below the psychological $100,000 mark.If BTC reclaims the $93,000–$94,000 zone, analysts say a push toward $100,000 may follow — but weak momentum and inconsistent volume still cloud the short-term outlook.A decisive shift in sentiment, trading activity, and macro conditions will determine whether December breaks from tradition or delivers the year-end relief rally many are hoping for.

data.title: Bitcoin News Today: Bitcoin Dominance Breaks Historical Pattern as BTC Drops 36% in Fastest Correction of the Cycle
data.description: Bitcoin has now suffered its third 30%+ correction of the current cycle — but unlike previous downturns, Bitcoin dominance fell instead of rising, signaling that the largest cryptocurrency was hit harder than the broader market during the sell-off.BTC plunged to the $80,000 region late last week, marking a 36% drawdown from its October all-time high above $126,000. While widespread deleveraging gripped the crypto market, alternative cryptocurrencies showed unusual resilience relative to Bitcoin, directly impacting the dominance metric.Dominance Behavior Flips: Instead of Rising, BTC Share of the Market FellHistorically, Bitcoin dominance climbs sharply during market-wide risk-off events, as traders rotate out of altcoins and into BTC or stablecoins. That pattern held earlier in the year — until now.What typically happens:February–May “tariff tantrum” correction: Dominance surged from 58% to 65%2024 yen carry-trade unwind: Dominance climbed from 56% to 60%What happened this time:As Bitcoin fell from October highs to $80,000, dominance dropped from 61% to 58.5%Even after recovering above $90,000, dominance has only rebounded to just over 59%This is the opposite of how dominance usually behaves in a deep BTC correction.The takeaway:Bitcoin underperformed the broader crypto market during this decline — a rare break from established cycle behavior.A Faster, Sharper Bitcoin Drawdown Than Previous CyclesSpeed was another defining characteristic of this correction.The current drawdown lasted 47 days from peak to troughThe February–May “tariff tantrum” lasted 77 daysThe 2024 yen-driven correction stretched 146 daysThe unusually rapid decline amplified fear and contributed to atypical investor behavior, including capital rotating into selected altcoins and stablecoins rather than consolidating into Bitcoin.Why this matters heading into the cycle’s next phaseThis cycle's third major correction deviates from the first two in two key ways:Bitcoin dominance fell when it historically risesThe decline happened significantly faster than previous resetsThese factors raise a critical question for investors as Bitcoin approaches the tail end of its four-year cycle:Does falling dominance signal deeper structural weakness, or is it a temporary anomaly tied to leverage washouts and ETF-driven flows?With Bitcoin stabilizing above $90,000 and dominance only partially recovering, market participants are watching closely to determine whether this cycle’s pattern truly breaks — or whether BTC reasserts its position as macro conditions normalize.

data.title: Spot silver hit a new all-time high, breaking the previous record to $54.5 per ounce.
data.description: Spot silver broke through the high set on October 17, setting a new all-time record of $54.5 per ounce, a daily increase of over 2%. (Jinshi Data APP)

data.title: Opinion: Bitcoin is poised to rebound to $100,000, with mining stocks Cipher Mining and Terawaulf performing strongly.
data.description: BTIG analyst Jonathan Krinsky said in a client report on Wednesday that after experiencing a -36% peak-to-trough drop earlier this month, Bitcoin is now poised to continue its reflexive rebound, at least returning to $100,000. According to Coin Metrics, Bitcoin is currently trading at $92,451.30, up nearly 10% over the past five days, but still down 20% over the past month. The analyst noted that despite the recent pullback, cryptocurrency mining companies Cipher Mining and Terawaulf have performed exceptionally well during the cryptocurrency correction. Cipher Mining is up 35% since Monday, and Terawaulf is up 31%. Barclay's Crypto Mining Index has held its support level and is expected to have another 15% upside before encountering stronger resistance. Furthermore, ETH, the second-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, also appears poised to reclaim the $3,400 level after falling 24% over the past month, currently trading at $3,075.62, up nearly 13% over the past five days. Solana and XRP rose 12% and 15% respectively during the same period. (CNBC)

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Bitcoin Stalls at $92K, Can BTC Clear Resistance or Is Another Drop Coming?
data.description: Bitcoin’s rebound from last week’s multi-month low is losing steam near the $92,000 mark, but analysts say the short-term trend still favors the upside — if buyers can regain key levels and restore market participation.Bitcoin is trading around $91,400, consolidating within a narrow band after climbing more than 13% from its $80,000 bottom.Bitcoin Must Reclaim $94K–$95K to Flip the TrendBTC has spent the past several days in a tight range between $90,300 and $92,000, according to TradingView data.Swissblock, a private wealth manager, says the real breakdown began when Bitcoin lost its yearly open at $93,300, calling it a “true trend shift.”The firm now identifies two critical zones:• Defensive zone: $83,000–$85,000BTC must hold this area to prevent a deeper correction.• Reversal zone: $94,000–$95,000Only a clean reclaim of this range would flip the trend bullish again.Glassnode’s cost-basis heatmap reinforces this view. Roughly 500,000 BTC were accumulated between $93,000 and $96,000, forming a dense supply cluster that must be absorbed before Bitcoin can regain momentum.The next major barrier sits between $100,000 and $108,000, where new buyers from the last peak are likely to sell into strength.Glassnode summarized the setup:“Breaking above the top-buyers’ supply clusters is a key prerequisite for regaining momentum toward a new all-time high.”Spot Volume Weakens as Transfer Activity Falls 20%Price alone is not the obstacle. Participation is.Bitcoin’s on-chain transfer volume declined nearly 20% this week to $87 billion (7-day MA), showing weaker economic activity on the network.Spot exchange trading volume has fallen to $12.8 billion, well below the surges that historically accompanied breakout rallies.Notably, BTC’s push back above $91,000 happened without a corresponding rise in spot activity, signaling reduced investor appetite and limited speculative energy.This divergence suggests Bitcoin lacks the trading momentum required to clear the $92,000–$95,000 resistance band.CVD Signals Early Signs of RecoveryDespite sluggish spot volume, market structure may be shifting.Glassnode data shows that Bitcoin’s taker cumulative volume delta (CVD) — an indicator of aggressive buying vs. selling — has moved back toward neutral after spending weeks in negative territory.If CVD turns decisively buyer-dominant, analysts expect a recovery phase similar to May–July, when BTC climbed 32% to its former all-time high of $123,000.What Bitcoin Must Do NextFor Bitcoin to avoid another sharp drawdown and re-establish a path toward six-figure prices, three conditions must align:Reclaim $94,000–$95,000 to confirm a trend reversal.Break $97,000–$98,000, which traders see as the first true confirmation zone.Witness a decisive return of spot market volume supporting a move to $100,000.Until then, Bitcoin remains range-bound and vulnerable to renewed selling pressure.

data.title: Stablecoin Market Value Sees Growth, Led by USDC
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, data from DefiLlama indicates that the total market capitalization of stablecoins has reversed its downward trend over the past week. The circulating market value is now reported at $305.578 billion, marking an increase of 0.8%.

USDC has been identified as the primary contributor to this growth, with its circulating market value rising by 3.31% over the past week.

data.title: Ethereum Supply Concentration Increases Among Top Addresses
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from Glassnode indicates that as of Wednesday, the top 1% of addresses hold 97.6% of the Ethereum supply, an increase from 96.1% a year ago.

Despite this concentration, Iliya Kalchev, a scheduling analyst at digital asset platform Nexo, notes that the market reaction remains "restrained." Stable capital inflows and rising derivatives trading activity suggest that investors are selectively rebuilding portfolios rather than actively rotating across the financial system.

data.title: 35% Bitcoin Correction And Hash Ribbon Bottom Signal Put SUBBD Token On Trader Radar
data.description:
What to Know: Hash Ribbon recovery after a 35% Bitcoin correction, coupled with depressed hashprice, aligns with historic late‑stage capitulation and potential cyclical bottoms. Miner stress and hashrate pullbacks often precede cleaner supply dynamics, setting the stage for capital rotations into higher‑beta narratives like AI and creator tokens. AI‑powered creator platforms are targeting opaque fees, arbitrary bans, and fragmented tooling as Web2 subscription and fan platforms continue to extract oversized revenue shares. SUBBD combines Web3 payments with AI assistants, voice cloning, and token‑gated content to help creators keep more earnings and automate fan engagement
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin-hash-ribbon-bottom-subbd-token-creator-economy/

data.description: According to Odaily, Arthur Hayes has predicted that by the end of 2026, the pricing of the largest U.S. stocks will transition to blockchain platforms. He suggests that the market focus will shift from the Nasdaq to perpetual contract charts, facilitated by 24/7, global, and intermediary-free trading.

data.title: Ethereum's Wealthiest Investors Continue Accumulation Amid Market Downturn
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, while some early Ethereum investors are cashing out, the wealthiest Ether holders are quietly accumulating the cryptocurrency despite the market's challenges. An Ethereum initial coin offering (ICO) participant recently sold $60 million worth of Ether, achieving a remarkable 9,500-fold return on investment over 11 years. This investor initially purchased Ether at approximately $0.31 per token, spending $79,000 on 254,000 tokens, which are now valued at over $757 million, as reported by blockchain data platform Lookonchain.

Following this sale, the ICO participant's wallet, identified as '0x2Eb,' retained $9.3 million in Ether, according to crypto intelligence platform Nansen. While some in the crypto community commend the investor's patience, others express concern that such profit-taking might signal potential market declines. However, the activity of this investor does not suggest panic selling but rather a consistent strategy of profit-taking, as the wallet has been gradually reducing its holdings since early September.

Despite these developments, the top 1% of Ether holders continue their accumulation undeterred by the market downturn. The proportion of Ether held by these addresses increased to 97.6% on Wednesday, up from 96.1% a year ago, according to blockchain data platform Glassnode. Additionally, U.S. spot Ether exchange-traded funds (ETFs) have resumed their accumulation, recovering after eight consecutive days of net outflows. These ETFs recorded $60 million in net positive inflows on Wednesday, marking the fourth consecutive day of gains, as noted by Farside Investors.

Despite the constructive inflows into Ether ETFs and the upcoming Ethereum Fusaka upgrade, the market's response remains measured, according to Iliya Kalchev, a dispatch analyst at digital asset platform Nexo. Kalchev explained that the combination of steady inflows and increased derivatives activity indicates that investors are selectively rebuilding exposure rather than aggressively rotating across the market. This cautious approach reflects a strategic rebuilding of positions amid ongoing market fluctuations.
